messages.content: <image>What is the size and type of the polyp observed in the gastrointestinal tract?
Polyp measures less than 5 millimeters with a sessile morphology
<image>Are there any signs of oesophagitis, polyps, or other abnormalities present in the image?
No polyps are visible, and there is no evidence of oesophagitis or other abnormalities.
<image>Is there any abnormality visible in this image?
no significant findings noted

No polyps are visualized in the gastrointestinal tract.
<image>Are there any instruments or anatomical landmarks visible in the image?
No foreign instruments identified, z-line visible
<image>Is there any remaining polyp tissue present?
No residual polyp tissue detected

No significant abnormality noted

no polypoidal lesions identified

No surgical instruments are present, and no anatomical landmark is identifiable.
<image>Have all polyps been removed, is there a green/black box artefact, and where is the instrument located in the image?
No evidence of residual polyps, no green or black box artefact present, and the instrument is positioned in the center-left and upper-left regions.
<image>Are there any abnormalities visible in the image? Is there any text present?
Evidence of a single polyp noted. Text is present.

evidence of black and green box artifacts

A tube is present in the central and upper-left regions of the image with text visible.
